info.title: Corbado Backend API
info.description:
# Introduction
This documentation gives an overview of all Corbado Backend API calls to implement passwordless authentication with Passkeys.

post.description:
Creates a new user with the given status. Use [login identifiers](/api-reference/backend-api/identifiers/create-a-login-identifier-for-a-user) to add an email address or phone number to the user.

You can also manage users in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/users).

get.description:
Returns a list of project users.

The list supports pagination and sorting:
- The `sort` parameter only supports the `created` field (use `created` for ascending, `-created` for descending).
- Users can be filtered by:
  - Single user ID using `userID`
  - Multiple user IDs using `userIDs` (max 1000)
  - Multiple WebAuthn IDs using `webauthnIDs` (max 1000)
  - Single identifier using `identifierValue` and `identifierType` together
  - Multiple identifiers using `identifierValues` and `identifierType` together (max 1000)

Filtering rules:
- Only one filter type can be used at a time: `userID`, `userIDs`, `webauthnIDs`, or identifier filters
- `userID` and `userIDs` are mutually exclusive
- `identifierValue` and `identifierValues` are mutually exclusive
- When filtering by identifier(s), `identifierType` must be provided

parameters.name: userID
parameters.description: Filter by specific user ID (format `usr-<number>`). Cannot be used together with `userIDs`.

parameters.name: userIDs
parameters.description: Filter by multiple user IDs (comma-separated). Cannot be used together with `userID`, `webauthnIDs`, or identifier filters. Maximum 1000 IDs.

parameters.name: userIDPattern
parameters.description: Filter by user IDs matching the given pattern. Supports `%` as a wildcard character. Cannot be used together with other filter types.

parameters.name: webauthnIDs
parameters.description: Filter by multiple WebAuthn IDs (comma-separated). Cannot be used together with other filter types. Maximum 1000 IDs.

parameters.name: identifierValue
parameters.description: Filter by identifier value (email, phone, or username). Must be used together with `identifierType`. Cannot be used together with `identifierValues`.

parameters.name: identifierValues
parameters.description: Filter by multiple identifier values (comma-separated). Must be used together with `identifierType`. Cannot be used together with `identifierValue`. Maximum 1000 values.

parameters.name: identifierType
parameters.description: Type of identifier to filter by. Must be used together with `identifierValue` or `identifierValues`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by user status.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sort field. Only `created` is supported.
- Use `+created` for ascending order
- Use `-created` for descending order

parameters.name: includeIdentifiers
parameters.description: Whether to include login identifiers in the response. Defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve for paginated results.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page. Useful for pagination.

get.description:
Retrieves a user by given `userID`. This does not return login identifiers like email addresses or phone numbers.

You can also manage users in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/users).

patch.description:
Updates a user by given `userID`. For example, this can be used to modify the user's status.

You can also manage users in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/users).

delete.description:
Deletes a user by given `userID`.

You can also manage users in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/users).

post.summary: Create a challenge for a user
post.description:
Creates a new challenge to verify a login identifier for a user by given `userID`. Challenges come in three flavors: **Email OTP**, **SMS OTP**, and **Email Magiclink**.

**OTP** stands for One-Time Password. It is a unique code sent to the user via email or SMS, which they must enter to complete the verification process.

patch.summary: Update a challenge for a user
patch.description: Updates a challenge for a user by given `userID` and `challengeID`. For example, this can be used to change the challenge status from `pending` to `completed`.

post.summary: Create a login identifier for a user
post.description:
Creates a new login identifier for a user with the given ID. Login identifiers can be of the type `email`, `phone`, or `username`. Separating login identifiers from users allows Corbado to manage multiple login identifiers of different types for each user.

You can set up login identifiers in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/settings/userinterface?tab=Overview) or consult the [Documentation](/corbado-complete/overview/configuration/user-flow-configuration#2-login-identifiers-and-fallbacks) for more details.

delete.summary: Delete a login identifier for a user
delete.description: Delete an existing login identifier for a user by given `userID` and `identifierID`.

patch.summary: Update a login identifier for a user
patch.description: Updates a login identifier (e.g. from `pending` to `verified`) for a user by given `userID` and `identifierID`.

get.summary: List all social logins for a user
get.description:
Returns a list of social logins for a user by given `userID`.

The list can be sorted and filtered:  
- The `sort` parameter supports the following fields: `providerType` and `foreignID`.
- The `filter` parameter supports the following fields: `providerType` and `foreignID`.

Refer to the parameter description for more details on sorting in different directions and using the filter with different operators.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Field and direction to sort results. Use the format `fieldName:asc` or `fieldName:desc`.

parameters.name: filter[]
parameters.description:
Filter results by specific fields and conditions. Format: `<field>:<operator>:<value>`.
Supported operators include:  
  - `eq`: equals (e\.g\. `email:eq:mail@example\.com` matches items where email equals mail@example\.com)  
  - `gt`: greater than (e\.g\. `created:gt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created after Jan 1, 2021)  
  - `lt`: less than (e\.g\. `created:lt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created before Jan 1, 2021)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve for paginated results.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page. Useful for pagination.

post.summary: Create a social login for a user
post.description:
Creates a new social login for a user by given `userID`. Social logins are used to authenticate users with third-party providers like Google, Microsoft, or GitHub.

You can set up social logins in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/settings/userinterface?tab=Social) or consult the [Documentation](/corbado-complete/overview/configuration/social-logins/overview) for more details.

get.description:
Returns a list of passkeys for a user by given `userID`.

The list can be sorted and filtered:
- The `sort` parameter supports the following fields: `userID`, `created`, `status`, `backupState`, `browserName` and `osName`.
- The `filter` parameter supports the following fields: `userID`, `created`, `status`, `backupState`, `browserName` and `osName`.

Refer to the parameter description for more details on sorting in different directions and using the filter with different operators.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Field and direction to sort results. Use the format `fieldName:asc` or `fieldName:desc`.

parameters.name: filter[]
parameters.description:
Filter results by specific fields and conditions. Format: `<field>:<operator>:<value>`.
Supported operators include:  
  - `eq`: equals (e\.g\. `email:eq:mail@example\.com` matches items where email equals mail@example\.com)  
  - `gt`: greater than (e\.g\. `created:gt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created after Jan 1, 2021)  
  - `lt`: less than (e\.g\. `created:lt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created before Jan 1, 2021)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve for paginated results.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page. Useful for pagination.

delete.summary: Delete a passkey for a user
delete.description: Deletes an existing passkey for a user by given `userID` and `credentialID`.

post.description: Create a new authentication event for a user

post.summary: Create a passkey event for a user
post.description: Creates a new passkey event for a user by given `userID`.

get.summary: List all passkey events for a user
get.description:
Returns a list of passkey events for a user by given `userID`.

The list can be sorted and filtered:
- The `sort` parameter supports the following fields: `created` and `eventType`.
- The `filter` parameter supports the following fields: `eventType`.

Refer to the parameter description for more details on sorting in different directions and using the filter with different operators.

parameters.name: sort

  - `lt`: less than (e\.g\. `created:lt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created before Jan 1, 2021)

200.description: List of all matching passkey events.

delete.summary: Delete a passkey event for a user
delete.description: Deletes an existing passkey event for a user by given `userID` and `passkeyEventID`.

get.summary: List all passkey challenges for a user
get.description:
Returns a list of passkey challenges for a user by given `userID`.

The list can be sorted and filtered:
- The `sort` parameter supports the following fields: `userID`, `projectID`, `status`, and `type`.
- The `filter` parameter supports the following fields: `userID`, `projectID`, `status`, and `type`.

Refer to the parameter description for more details on sorting in different directions and using the filter with different operators.

put.summary: Update project CNAME and generate SSL certificate
put.description: Update project config CNAME and generates new SSL certificate

post.description:
Creates a new ConnectToken, which is essential for [Corbado Connect](/corbado-connect). ConnectTokens authorize actions that modify user data.

Consult the [Documentation](/corbado-connect/concepts/connect-token) for more details.

get.description:
Returns a list of ConnectTokens.

The list can be sorted and filtered:
- The `sort` parameter supports the following fields: `token`, `tokenType`, `status` and `expires`.
- The `filter` parameter supports the following fields: `token`, `tokenType`, `status` and `expires`.

Refer to the parameter description for more details on sorting in different directions and using the filter with different operators.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Field and direction to sort results. Use the format `fieldName:asc` or `fieldName:desc`.

parameters.name: filter[]
parameters.description:
Filter results by specific fields and conditions. Format: `<field>:<operator>:<value>`.
Supported operators include:  
  - `eq`: equals (e\.g\. `email:eq:mail@example\.com` matches items where email equals mail@example\.com)  
  - `gt`: greater than (e\.g\. `created:gt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created after Jan 1, 2021)  
  - `lt`: less than (e\.g\. `created:lt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created before Jan 1, 2021)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve for paginated results.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page. Useful for pagination.

200.description: List of all matching append tokens.

get.description: Returns a list of webhook endpoints

Returns a list of sessions.

The list can be sorted and filtered:
- The `sort` parameter supports the following fields: `ID`, `userID`, `expires`, `lastAction`, `created`, `updated`, and `status`.
- The `filter` parameter supports the following fields: `ID`, `userID`, `expires`, `lastAction`, `created`, `updated`, and `status`.

Refer to the parameter description for more details on sorting in different directions and using the filter with different operators.

You can view and manage sessions in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/users/sessions) or consult the [Documentation](https://docs.corbado.com/corbado-complete/sessions/overview) for more details.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Field and direction to sort results. Use the format `fieldName:asc` or `fieldName:desc`.

parameters.name: filter[]
parameters.description:
Filter results by specific fields and conditions. Format: `<field>:<operator>:<value>`.
Supported operators include:  
  - `eq`: equals (e\.g\. `email:eq:mail@example\.com` matches items where email equals mail@example\.com)  
  - `gt`: greater than (e\.g\. `created:gt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created after Jan 1, 2021)  
  - `lt`: less than (e\.g\. `created:lt:2021-01-01T00:00:00` matches items created before Jan 1, 2021)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve for paginated results.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page. Useful for pagination.

post.description: Revokes an existing session by given `sessionID`.

parameters.name: sessionID
parameters.description: ID of session

post.description: Starts creating a new passkey by initiating the WebAuthn ceremony. To complete the ceremony you need to call [finish](/api-reference/backend-api/passkeys/finish-creating-a-new-passkey).

200.description: Passkey creation process has been started.

post.description: Finishes creating a new passkey by completing the WebAuthn ceremony.

post.summary: Start login with an existing passkey
post.description: Starts login with an existing passkey by initiating the WebAuthn ceremony. To complete the ceremony you need to call [finish](/api-reference/backend-api/passkeys/finish-login-with-an-existing-passkey).

200.description: Passkey authentication process has been started.

post.summary: Finish login with an existing passkey
post.description: Finishes login with an existing passkey by completing the WebAuthn ceremony.
Starts SAML2-based SSO login by resolving the project's SSO configuration for the email and returning an IdP redirect URL.
Finishes SAML2-based SSO login by validating the SAML response against the pending SSO session and resolving/provisioning a user.

post.summary: Run the post-login action for passkey
Verify signedPasskeyData from a passkey login
post.description:
Verifies the `signedPasskeyData` from a passkey login. The `signedPasskeyData` is returned by the [finish](/api-reference/backend-api/passkeys/finish-login-with-an-existing-passkey) call and is essential for transferring the authentication state to your backend.

It is required only for [Corbado Connect](/corbado-connect) and is thoroughly explained in our [Documentation](/corbado-connect/concepts/signed-passkey-data). Our [generic integration guide](/corbado-connect/integration/generic) explains how to use `signedPasskeyData` in your backend.

post.summary: Start login with an existing passkey (Conditional UI)
post.description: Starts login with an existing passkey in [Conditional UI](https://www.corbado.com/glossary/conditional-ui) scenario. To complete the ceremony you need to call [finish](/api-reference/backend-api/passkeys/finish-login-with-an-existing-passkey-conditional-ui).

200.description: Passkey login process has been started.

post.summary: Finish login with an existing passkey (Conditional UI)
post.description: Finishes login with an existing passkey in [Conditional UI](https://www.corbado.com/glossary/conditional-ui) scenario.

200.description: Passkey login process has been completed.

basicAuth.description:
Basic authentication is used to authenticate requests to the Backend API. The username is the project ID and the password is the API secret.

The project ID and API secret can be found in the [Developer Panel](https://app.corbado.com/settings/api-secrets).

userID.name: userID
userID.description: Unique identifier of the user. Format: `usr-<number>`.

identifierID.name: identifierID
identifierID.description: Unique identifier of the login identifier (e.g., email address or phone number). Format: `ide-<number>`.

credentialID.name: credentialID
credentialID.description: Unique identifier of the passkey. Format: `cre-<number>`.

connectTokenID.name: connectTokenID
connectTokenID.description: Unique identifier of the connectToken. Format: `ctk-<number>`.

includeAllStatus.name: includeAllStatus
includeAllStatus.description: Whether to include all status types (including `deleted`) in the response. Default is `false`, which excludes `deleted` items.

explicitWebauthnID.description: For [Corbado Connect](https://docs.corbado.com/corbado-connect) projects, the webauthnID can be explicitly set for a user

maxLifetimeInSeconds.description: Maximum lifetime of the connectToken in seconds.

identifierValue.description: Login identifier of the user (here email address).

createdMs.description: Unix time of when the session was created (in milliseconds elapsed since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC).

lastActionMs.description: Unix time of when last action (e.g., refresh) on session occurred (in milliseconds elapsed since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC).

expiresMs.description: Unix time of when the session expires (in milliseconds elapsed since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC).

identifierID.description: Unique identifier of the login identifier.

value.description: Value of the identifier (here email address).

identifierValue.description: Login identifier of the user (here email address).

foreignID.description: Unique identifier of the user in the social provider.

avatarURL.description: URL of the avatar of the user in the social provider.

fullName.description: Full name of the user in the social provider.

socialAccountID.description: Unique identifier of the social account.

identifierValue.description: Login identifier of the user (here email address).

foreignID.description: Unique identifier of the user in the social provider.

avatarURL.description: URL of the avatar of the user in the social provider.

fullName.description: Full name of the user in the social provider.

lastUsed.description: Timestamp of when the passkey was last used in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss format

created.description: Timestamp of when the entity was created in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss format
Timestamp of when the entity was created in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss format
Timestamp of when the entity was created in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss format

userAgent.example: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/104.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

expires.description: Unix time of when the connectToken expires (in seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC).

identifier.description: Login identifier of the user (here email address).
Login identifier of the user (here email address).
Login identifier of the user (here email address).
Login identifier of the user (here email address).

requestData.description: Data about the request itself, can be used for debugging

requestID.description: Unique ID of request, you can provide your own while making the request, if not the ID will be randomly generated on server side

link.description: Link to dashboard with details about request

runtime.description: Runtime in seconds for this request

links.description: Additional links to help understand the error
